summary: Import-time credential and browser-data exfiltration to Telegram bot
details: On import, request_cache_py/__init__.py spawns a background thread that harvests installer secrets and posts them to a hardcoded Telegram bot endpoint. Targeted paths include ~/.ssh/id_rsa, id_ed25519, id_ecdsa, id_dsa; ~/.aws/credentials and ~/.aws/config; ~/.gitconfig and ~/.git-credentials; ~/.npmrc; ~/.pypirc; ~/.docker/config.json; gcloud credentials; and VSCode settings. It also copies Chrome/Edge/Safari Cookies, Login Data, and History SQLite databases to temp files and runs queries such as 'SELECT host_key, name, value, path FROM cookies LIMIT 100' and 'SELECT origin_url, username_value FROM logins LIMIT 50' to extract saved cookies and credentials. Environment variables matching key/token/secret/password/api/auth are also collected. The destination Telegram bot token (8906743150:AAG8f0NQQDk5rVZNIIWwqYL6LlLg_RbmFqc) and chat ID (8276546637) are reassembled at runtime from split base64 fragments inside _get_endpoint() to evade static analysis, and a _should_skip() guard plus a one-shot marker file are used for sandbox/CI evasion. The package name mimics the legitimate `requests-cache` library and advertises itself as 'High-performance HTTP request caching with Redis and in-memory backends', while the metadata author is a generic placeholder ('Python Performance Team', perf-team@pythonutils.org). Any environment that imports this module will leak SSH private keys, cloud credentials, registry tokens, and browser session cookies to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
